title: Heatwave fuels frustration in Paris's northern suburbs: 'If they'd just given us shutters, we wouldn't be suffering like this'
subhead: Over the past week, daily life in two large concrete housing complexes in Seine-Saint-Denis has changed dramatically due to extreme heat. Residents share their struggles and anger as they try to help each other cope.

title: Dayot Upamecano, the leader of the French defense, faces the Haaland menace at the 2026 World Cup
subhead: Upamecano, who plays for Bayern Munich, faces the daunting task of limiting Manchester City striker Erling Haaland's influence on Friday, in the high-stakes match between France and Norway.

title: 250 years of American independence: When John Adams predicted that democracy would commit suicide
subhead: 'America 250' (5/13). John Adams, the second president of the United States, and his successor Thomas Jefferson did not share the same view of human nature. Adams, an educated anti-slavery advocate from the North, feared that democracy would perish under the weight of corruption among the powerful. Posterity, however, has largely remembered the soaring rhetoric of Jefferson, the slaveholding Southern planter.

subhead: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.

title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.
